items.title: Medical care of Floreat double-murderer to be focus of inquest

children.content: The main focus of a coronial inquest into the death of a Perth mother and her daughter will be the medical care their killer Mark Bombara received in the lead up to the double-murder suicide, a coroner reveals.

gallery.altText: Jennifer and Gretl Petelczyc who were shot dead in their own home by Mark James Bombara.

thumbnail.altText: Jennifer and Gretl Petelczyc who were shot dead in their own home by Mark James Bombara.

items.title: New line of hope in discovering the Nannup Four

children.content: The vice-president of a search team that solved two high-profile missing persons cases in Tasmania turns his attention to the Nannup Four case after it was featured in the Expanse podcast.

image.altText: A photo of two men, a woman and a girl with a photo of bushland in the background.

items.title: After 70 bags of fruit were stolen in one weekend, farmer closes roadside stall

children.content: Mandarin grower Bruce Crain says people's growing dishonesty, including the theft of 70 bags in one weekend, makes it impossible to offer a roadside service.

image.altText: Several bags of mandarins on a black table with a sign reading "$5 per bag" placed inside of a corrugated steel shelter.

items.title: Pauline Hanson reveals Gina Rinehart's role in One Nation policy making

children.content: Pauline Hanson speaks of her "respect" for billionaire Gina Rinehart, becoming emotional during a speech in Perth recalling her time in detention.

articles.title: Billionaire backer Rinehart dictates to Hanson, Chalmers says

children.content: Treasurer Jim Chalmers hits out at Pauline Hanson's ties to Gina Rinehart and questions whether she actually stands for battling Australians.

image.altText: Treasurer Jim Chalmers in a navy suit and maroon tie, pressing his lips tightly.

articles.title: Hundreds protest against Pauline Hanson as WA Labor seats on 'hit list'

children.content: A crowd of about 400 protesters demonstrates against One Nation's Pauline Hanson as she speaks at a sundowner event in Perth's eastern suburbs.

image.altText: A group of protesters holding anti-Pauline Hanson signs.

gallery.altText: Pauline Hanson speaks to reporters among a crowd, wearing a brown and black jacket.
Four people stand inside a shed with three holding big novelty cheques.

thumbnail.altText: Pauline Hanson speaks to reporters among a crowd, wearing a brown and black jacket.
Four people stand inside a shed with three holding big novelty cheques.

items.title: Beachside towns in WA face difficult decisions as erosion gets worse

children.content: A week after Western Australia experienced one of its worst storms in decades, some stretches of coast are crumbling into the ocean at an alarming rate. Millions of dollars' worth of infrastructure is at risk heading into winter.

gallery.altText: Sand bags sit in front a cliff of sand. A building sits a few metres behind.
A drone shot shows the ocean and erosion near a road. Behind it sits a recreation centre, playground, bike track and houses.
Glen Trebilcock lifts a shovel full of sand, in front of a fork lift holding an open sand bag

thumbnail.altText: Sand bags sit in front a cliff of sand. A building sits a few metres behind.
A drone shot shows the ocean and erosion near a road. Behind it sits a recreation centre, playground, bike track and houses.
Glen Trebilcock lifts a shovel full of sand, in front of a fork lift holding an open sand bag

items.title: NDIS spending cuts would see 240,000 people exit the scheme, inquiry told

children.content: More than 240,000 people would exit the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) over four years according to modelling as part of a plan to cut spending on the multi-billion-dollar program.

image.altText: Jenny mcalliser appearing at Estimates Committee

items.title: Angus Taylor brushes away questions on One Nation preference deal

children.content: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor has brushed away questions on a preference deal with One Nation. Follow live.

image.altText: A man in a navy suit and tie looks sternly into camera.

items.title: How One Nation could disrupt Australia's progressive stronghold

children.content: The idea that One Nation could come close to the government benches was absurd six months ago, but it is a prospect Victorian Labor MPs and insiders are increasingly discussing.

image.altText: pauline hanson as seen from the crowd as she addresses bbq attendees

avatar.alt: Richard Willingham in front of a white background, stares straight down the barrel.

items.title: One Nation's Melbourne fundraiser moved over protest concerns

children.content: A One Nation fundraiser in Melbourne has been moved to an undisclosed location after several protest groups vowed to demonstrate outside, the party says.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson Barnaby Joyce

items.title: Quick hits: Opening ceremony delights, drinks break drama and Haiti's jersey ban

children.content: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is up and running, launched by a colourful opening ceremony and comprehensive win for Mexico. Here are the quick hits from the opening day of the tournament.

articles.title: South Korea completes Czechia comeback, relief for Socceroos star

children.content: The 2026 FIFA World Cup gets underway as South Korea comes from a goal down to beat Czechia 2-1 and Mexico defeats South Africa 2-0 in an opening game which features three red cards.

image.altText: South Korea's Hwang In-beom is hugged by Seol Young-woo in celebration of a goal

articles.title: Socceroos coach Tony Popovic signs extension through to Asian Cup

children.content: Socceroos coach Tony Popovic gets his contract extended just days before Australia's World Cup kicks off to take the national team through to next year's Asian Cup.

image.altText: Socceroos coach Tony Popovic gestures to his team, holding his arm out in a halting motion during a game.

articles.title: The head injury that almost ended it all for Mexico's 'miracle' man

children.content: In 2020, Mexico World Cup goal scorer Raúl Jiménez was lying on the turf of the Arsenal Stadium receiving oxygen after fracturing his skull and injuring his brain.

image.altText: Raul Jimenez points to the sky

gallery.altText: A shot showing fireworks, dancers and a giant replica World Cup trophy during the Opening Ceremony

thumbnail.altText: A shot showing fireworks, dancers and a giant replica World Cup trophy during the Opening Ceremony

items.title: BHP bracing for strike after WA workers back industrial action

children.content: Unionised workers at BHP's operations in Port Hedland could walk off the job as soon as next week after endorsing protected industrial action.

image.altText: A water tank with the words 'Welcome to Port Hedland' and the BHP logo

items.title: What are the rules around using fire pits at home?

children.content: There are rules when it comes to using fire pits and things to be cautious about, including open-air fires being a nuisance to your neighbours.

image.altText: fire pit in the backyard

items.title: New species found in Australia's most 'pristine' marine parks

children.content: Researchers have catalogued dozens of new marine species found in the depths of Australia's most remote waters. The result is a window into a rarely glimpsed world.

gallery.altText: A close-up image of an oval-shaped, soft-bodied organism with 10 legs.
A yellow sea star sits on black background next to a white scientific label.

thumbnail.altText: A close-up image of an oval-shaped, soft-bodied organism with 10 legs.
A yellow sea star sits on black background next to a white scientific label.

items.title: 'Broken promise' or not, Albanese's tax decision helps One Nation

children.content: The prime minister's cavalier breaking of election promises has poured kerosene on the fire under a party that can no longer be called "minor".

image.altText: Prime minister Anthony Albanese

items.title: How you and your superannuation came to be aboard SpaceX

children.content: If your superannuation is managed by any of Australia's big superannuation funds, chances are you're about to become a highly valued financial backer to Elon Musk.

image.altText: smoke and flames emerge from a spacex launch in texas

items.title: Town abandoned by gas company faces tough choices as transition hits home

children.content: For many, getting off gas and electrifying their homes is a personal choice. But what happens when an entire town is abandoned by a gas company?

gallery.altText: Bearded man wearing black polo shirt standing at a stainless steel counter in a professional kitchen
Drone shot of Albany with harbour and marina in the foreground
A 'danger' sign warning of a high pressure buried gas pipeline.

Drone shot of Albany with harbour and marina in the foreground
A 'danger' sign warning of a high pressure buried gas pipeline.

items.title: Government agencies fail first hurdle under AI self-reporting policy

children.content: The ABC can reveal dozens of federal agencies missed mandatory deadlines to disclose how they are currently using AI.

image.altText: A woman's hands type on a laptop keyboard.

items.title: Amex ordered to compensate man whose ex accessed his data

children.content: Australia's privacy commissioner has threatened a complainant with legal action to prevent the full disclosure of the findings of a long-running investigation into American Express's information security, which found widespread technology failures.

image.altText: A modern office building with an American Express sign.

items.title: More couples opt for micro-weddings and elopements amid cost-of-living crisis

children.content: Melbourne couple Averill and Lloyd Johnson pulled off their dream wedding for just $3,000, compared to the average wedding cost of about $38,000.

image.altText: Groom wearing a suit and top hat and bridge wearing a black dress, holding up their middle fingers with rings on them.

items.title: Study reveals complex links between social media use and teen wellbeing

children.content: A decade-long study following almost 1,200 Melbourne school students finds a "small increased risk" of depressive symptoms for teenagers using social media at least two hours a day.

image.altText: teens using their phones with various social media related illustrations

items.title: WA muso's 3D-printed instruments draw on decades of creativity

children.content: After a music career that's taken him around the world, Eddy Jay has turned to cutting-edge manufacturing to make his own instruments.

image.altText: A medium of man holding a concertina

gallery.altText: A medium of man holding a concertina

thumbnail.altText: A medium of man holding a concertina

items.title: Major PFAS class action could lead to multi-billion dollar claim figure

children.content: Hundreds of businesses and organisations are part of a major Victorian Supreme Court class action against chemical manufacturer 3M.

image.altText: An industrial plant in the Melbourne suburb of Altona.

items.title: Friday news quiz: Fake weather, a whale graveyard and James Bond going woke

children.content: Don't let your confidence be shaken or stirred by this week's test of how much you've been paying attention.

image.altText: Daniel Craig, as James Bond, stands next to his Aston Martin in a scene from Skyfall.

items.title: Massive whale graveyard found in deep ocean off Australia

children.content: Hundreds of whale fossils, including those from extinct species new to science, range in age from recent to 5 million years old and are also home to undescribed deep-sea creatures.

gallery.altText: Underwater vision of a whale fossil on the seafloor.

thumbnail.altText: Underwater vision of a whale fossil on the seafloor.
